# ThreeNative AI Reference

ThreeNative is a TypeScript game SDK with a Three.js-like authoring surface,
validated portable IR, and runtime adapters for web Three.js and native Bevy.
The stable contract is:

```txt
TypeScript authoring / structured source documents
  -> SDK object model and ECS declarations
  -> compiler extraction and validation
  -> versioned IR bundle
  -> web Three.js preview or native Bevy runtime adapter
```

## Packages

| Package | Role | Public entrypoints |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `@threenative/sdk` | TypeScript authoring API. | `.` |
| `@threenative/ir` | Bundle schemas, validation, capabilities, diagnostics. | `.`, `./bundlePaths`, `./conformance`, `./input`, `./reflection`, `./runtimeDiagnostics`, `./schemas/*`, `./capabilities/*`, `./diagnostics/*` |
| `@threenative/compiler` | Build project source into a portable bundle. | `.` |
| `@threenative/cli` | User commands and generated project templates. | `tn` bin |
| `@threenative/runtime-web-three` | Web runtime adapter. | `.` |
| `@threenative/ui` | Portable retained UI authoring helpers. | `.`, `./jsx-runtime` |
| `@threenative/r3f` | React/R3F capture helpers. | `.`, `./jsx-runtime` |

## Core Commands

```bash
tn create simple-game --template starter
tn build
tn verify --json
tn scene validate arena --json
tn scene inspect arena --json
tn scene proof arena --project . --web-url http://127.0.0.1:5173 --out artifacts/proof --json
tn editor dev --project . --json
```

## Metadata For Agents

- JSON Schemas: import or read files under
  `@threenative/ir/schemas/*.schema.json`.
- Capability manifest:
  `@threenative/ir/capabilities/threenative.capabilities.json`.
- Diagnostics catalog:
  `@threenative/ir/diagnostics/diagnostics.catalog.json`.
- Distribution contract: `docs/contracts/distribution-contract.md`.
- Diagnostics guide: `docs/contracts/diagnostics.md`.
- AI workflow guide: `docs/workflows/ai-distribution.md`.
- Canonical example index: `examples/ai-reference/README.md`.

Every schema-backed bundle document has `schema` and `version` fields. Use the
capability manifest before generating advanced renderer, physics, UI, audio,
navigation, scripting, or editor-source features. Use the diagnostics catalog to
repair failures by code and surface.

## Supported Authoring Pattern

```ts
import { BoxGeometry, Mesh, MeshStandardMaterial, Scene, defineGame, defineScene } from "@threenative/sdk";

const scene = new Scene();
scene.add(new Mesh(new BoxGeometry(1, 1, 1), new MeshStandardMaterial({ color: "#2f80ed" })));

export default defineGame({
  scenes: [defineScene("arena", scene)],
});
```

Prefer package declarations and the exported metadata over repository source.
When mutating scene data, prefer `tn scene ... --json` and structured source
documents over hand-written generated bundle JSON.

## Unsupported Boundaries

- Raw Three.js source is not the durable source of truth.
- Raw Bevy authoring is not public API.
- Browser DOM, worker, timer, filesystem, native handles, renderer plugins, and
  platform APIs are not portable unless a ThreeNative facade declares support.
- Generated bundle files such as `world.ir.json`, `manifest.json`, and
  `scripts.bundle.js` are build outputs, not editor-owned source.
- Backend-only, multiplayer, online services, 2D tilemap/sprite, custom shader,
  and arbitrary plugin workflows require explicit capability support before use.
